title: Dataset and Scripts for the article: "Estimation of Carbon Use Efficiency by the 18O method: the incidence of microbial turnover assessment"
author: Rodríguez, Antonio
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This dataset and scripts correspond to the experiments carried on the article titled "Estimation of Carbon Use Efficiency by the 18O method: the incidence of microbial turnover assessment". 
 We investigated four aspects of the  18 O method for the estimation of microbial carbon use efficiency (CUE) that vary in literature. Briefly, this method consists on incubating soil with  18 O-H 2 O and the estimating microbial growth by measuring of the incorporation of  18 O to DNA. 
 Specifically, we 
 1) Assessed the sensitivity of CUE to the different measurements needed for its calculation; 
 2) Simulated the number of replicas needed to detect CUE increases in various soils; 
 3) Evaluated the potential impacts of the effects of the two most used DNA extraction kits of the CUE values; and 
 4) Investigated the risks associated with spiking DNA extracts with different salmon DNA quantities. 
   
 Each of these questions was adressed in an experiment, numered 1-4. Here we provide open access to the data and the R scripts used to obtain the main results. 
 Experiment 2 has no data as it is fully simulated. 
 Each excel file contains a "Codebook" with the variable list, description and units. 
 See the manuscript for the background and details about how data were collected.
